L1.area: Dynamic icon rendering and SVG content sanitization logic

L2.description: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled input is embedded into dynamically generated SVG output without proper escaping. The SVG sanitization routine only strips script elements but does not remove dangerous event handler attributes or unsafe embedded elements, allowing crafted payloads to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the application's web origin.

L3.area: Dynamic icon SVG generation endpoint and SVG sanitization utility
L3.description: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in an unauthenticated endpoint that generates dynamic SVG icons. When a specific icon type is requested, attacker-controlled content is interpolated directly into SVG text elements via string formatting without escaping. The SVG sanitization routine only removes script elements but fails to strip dangerous event handler attributes (such as onerror or onload) or unsafe embedded elements like iframes and foreign objects, allowing crafted payloads to execute arbitrary JavaScript.

ground_truth.reason: Attacker-controlled input is embedded into dynamically generated SVG output without escaping, and the sanitizer only removes <script> tags but not event handler attributes (onerror, onload, etc.) or other dangerous elements, enabling reflected XSS.
